message:
feat(denial): pattern_regex field on DenialBox + regex syntax highlighting

Adds an opt-in `Regex:` line to the denial UI so users can see exactly
which pattern matched, with proper syntax highlighting under TTYs that
support color. Production callers don't yet pass the regex through —
this commit establishes the rendering surface and the helper that
will color the displayed regex.

src/highlight.rs:
- New `format_regex_pattern(pattern: &str, use_color: bool) -> String`.
  When `use_color` is false or the pattern is empty, returns the
  pattern unchanged. Otherwise:
  - Behind `feature = "rich-output"`, try `rich_rust::Syntax` with a
    short alias list (`regex`, `re`, `Regular Expressions`) and a
    fallback theme list (`python-rich-default`,
    `base16-ocean.dark`, `InspiredGitHub`). Each combination is
    attempted in order; we trim trailing newlines and return the
    first non-empty rendering.
  - When the rich path is unavailable or the syntax set doesn't know
    regex, fall back to a manual highlighter that walks the pattern
    char-by-char and colors metacharacters: `\` is dimmed and the
    next char is bright_cyan; `^` / `$` are cyan bold; `*+?{}` are
    yellow bold (the rest of the manual rules live below the snippet
    shown in this hunk).
- The manual fallback preserves the pattern bytes — colors are added
  via terminal escape codes only.

src/output/denial.rs:
- New `pattern_regex: Option<String>` field on `DenialBox`, plus a
  `with_pattern_regex(impl Into<String>)` builder that trims and
  rejects empty patterns (so callers don't have to filter).
- `format_pattern_lines` now takes `pattern_regex: Option<&str>` and
  `use_color: bool` and, when a regex is present, appends a third
  line `"Regex: {format_regex_pattern(regex, use_color)}"` after the
  existing Pattern / Pack lines.
- Every render path (`render_rich`, `render_plain`, `render_modern`,
  `render_ascii`, `render_color`, etc.) is updated to pass the field
  through, with `use_color` set from the theme's `colors_enabled`
  for the colored renderers and `false` for `render_plain` and
  `render_ascii`.
- New test `test_denial_box_renders_pattern_regex_when_available`
  asserts that the Regex line shows up in plain output when the
  builder is used.

No production callers pass `with_pattern_regex` yet; this is the
plumbing for a follow-up that wires the matched pattern's regex
through from the packs layer.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
